name: Circles — Tangents and Chords
whatIsIt: A TANGENT is a line that touches a circle at exactly one point. Two big rules: (1) a tangent is always perpendicular (90°) to the radius at the point it touches; (2) the two tangents drawn from the same outside point are EQUAL in length. A CHORD is a line joining two points on the circle, and the perpendicular from the centre bisects (cuts in half) the chord.
whyNeeded: These rules turn circle problems into right-triangle problems you can solve with Pythagoras — finding the length of a belt around a pulley, the reach of a tangent line, or how far a chord sits from the centre. They show up in design, gears, and any wheel-and-rope setup.
realLifeUses: Length of a belt or rope touching a pulley (tangent)
How far a straight cut (chord) lies from a plate's centre
Designing gears and cams that touch at one point
Checking a road is tangent to a roundabout at the kerb

vocabulary.meaning: A line touching the circle at exactly one point; it is perpendicular to the radius there.
A straight line joining two points on the circle. The longest chord is the diameter.
The single point where a tangent touches the circle.
The two tangents from one external point to a circle have the same length.

story.title: The Kite String and the Wheel
story.text: Isha ties a string from a peg P to just touch a round wheel. The centre O is 10 cm from P, and the wheel's radius is 6 cm. 'How long is the touching part of the string?' Bhai reminds her the tangent meets the radius at 90°, making a right triangle OP–radius–tangent. By Pythagoras the tangent = √(10² − 6²) = √(100 − 36) = √64 = 8 cm. 'And if I draw a second tangent from P, it's also 8 cm,' Isha realises.
story.extractedProblem: From point P, distance OP = 10 cm to the centre, radius 6 cm. Find the tangent length from P.

visual.caption: The tangent meets the radius at 90°, so O, the point of contact and P form a right triangle: tangent = √(OP² − r²).

teachingGallery.title: Tangent, radius, right angle
teachingGallery.note: Radius to the point of contact is perpendicular to the tangent — that 90° lets Pythagoras in.

examples.caption: Both tangents from the same outside point are equal in length.

shapes.label: perpendicular from O bisects the chord

examples.caption: The perpendicular from the centre cuts a chord into two equal halves.

standardMethod.summary: Draw the radius to the point of contact (90° to the tangent). Use Pythagoras on the right triangle centre–contact–external point. For chords, drop a perpendicular from the centre to get two equal halves.
steps: Mark the radius to the point of contact — it is perpendicular to the tangent (90°).
For a tangent from an external point P: tangent² = OP² − r², so tangent = √(OP² − r²).
Two tangents from the same external point are equal — set their lengths equal.
For a chord: the perpendicular from the centre bisects it, so half-chord² + distance² = r².

workedExamples.problem: From P, OP = 10 cm and radius = 6 cm. Find the tangent length.
steps: Radius ⟂ tangent, so triangle O–contact–P is right-angled.

workedExamples.problem: A chord of length 16 cm is in a circle of radius 10 cm. How far is the chord from the centre?
steps: The perpendicular from the centre bisects the chord: half = 8 cm.

commonMistakes.mistake: Forgetting the radius meets the tangent at 90°, so not using a right triangle.
commonMistakes.fix: The radius to the point of contact is ALWAYS perpendicular to the tangent. That 90° is what lets you use Pythagoras.

commonMistakes.mistake: Using the full chord length instead of half when dropping the perpendicular from the centre.
commonMistakes.fix: The perpendicular from the centre bisects the chord. Use HALF the chord in r² = half² + distance².

easy.q: The angle between a tangent and the radius at the point of contact is how many degrees?
hintLadder: Tangent is perpendicular to the radius.

easy.q: One tangent from point P is 7 cm. The other tangent from the same point P is how long?
hintLadder: Two tangents from one point are equal.

challenge.q: A chord of length 16 sits in a circle of radius 10. Distance from centre = √(10² − 8²) = √36 = ?
To find a tangent from P, Dev uses OP² + r². What's the fix?

options.label: Use OP² − r² (the tangent is the shorter leg)

challenge.answer: Use OP² − r² (the tangent is the shorter leg)

questions.q: Angle between tangent and radius at the contact point?
Chord length 24 in a circle of radius 13. Distance from centre = √(169 − 144) = √25 = ?
The perpendicular from the centre to a chord…

options.label: Bisects the chord into two equal halves

questions.answer: Bisects the chord into two equal halves

teachBackPrompt: Draw a circle with a tangent from an outside point for a grown-up. Show the radius meeting the tangent at 90°, and use √(OP² − r²) to find the tangent length. Then show how the perpendicular from the centre cuts a chord in half.

revisionCard.summary: Tangent touches at one point and is ⟂ (90°) to the radius there → tangent = √(OP² − r²). Two tangents from one external point are equal. The perpendicular from the centre bisects a chord: r² = (half-chord)² + (distance from centre)².

rememberIt.hook: A tangent touches at ONE point, perpendicular to the radius there
rememberIt.unpack: Two tangents from an outside point are equal in length. Radius meets tangent at 90 degrees.

studentQuestions.q: What's special about a tangent and the radius?
studentQuestions.a: They meet at exactly 90 degrees at the point of contact - always a right angle.
They are equal in length - the point is the same distance along each.
